title: cuscuta reflexa var. verrucosa
intro: Cuscuta reflexa var. verrucosa, also known as dodder, is a parasitic plant that obtains its nutrients by attaching itself to a host plant and absorbing nutrients and water through its haustoria. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory effects. The health benefits of Cuscuta reflexa var. verrucosa include its ability to improve fertility, reduce stress and anxiety, and boost the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of tinctures, capsules, and teas, and it is also used in traditional Ayurvedic and Chinese medicine.
